### 🎯 Coverage Score: **100%** (5/5 signal types: news ✅ price_index ✅ freight ✅ supply_disruption ✅ geopolitical ✅)

**Bottom line:** The US PP homopolymer market is in a structural supply crisis, not a cyclical correction. The Strait of Hormuz closure + Jubail strike has removed a major slice of global supply, domestic tariff policy has walled off Asian import alternatives, and US Gulf Coast cracker constraints have tightened domestic propylene simultaneously. FAS Houston at $1,665/MT (~$0.76/lb) represents a ~55–60% premium over pre-conflict January 2026 levels. No near-term relief catalyst is visible.
